sentences.text: Who is going to see the video?
We got a hat, a special hat-- green.
And we have pullover, and jacket, stripes, and trousers, stripes, and shoes, but no underwear.
We get just-- I don't know what was it, you know?
I was unconscious two times [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] during the-- the-- the transport.
Because when [? it ?] 6 o'clock, they put us into wagons.
And everyone got three slices of bread.
And from-- from hunger I was two times unconscious, OK?
Eventually, we came to the factory.
I was laying on the straw hungry first day.
And the-- my friend went out, you know.
They got a piece of bread.
Next day in the morning, come on.
I think Schindler wanted to see you.
I went down because [INAUDIBLE] floor in fact.
Excuse me, what are you doing?
You should organize your job, OK?
It was like, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] oh, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] if you-- no, I-- a room with food.
In the-- in the corner was bread.
Take it and get-- get well.
I took a bread and potato, and I start to work, OK?
Take people because we were too many people.
You must-- they give the work.
But suddenly, the SS, they wanted to paint the rooms for them.
And so I start to be busy.
And I took another three or four people, you know?
I said, it was old man [?
Maybe 70. ?] How he came to us, I don't know.
You got-- when somebody's coming, paint.
The commander wanted to make friend.
This other guy, what, he killed thousands and thousands of Jews in Poland.
And in the camp, he got two rooms with kitchen, everything.
And he took one girl to cook, you know?
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] It was a murder.
And he suddenly-- where's the painter?
I need a painter because it's dirty.
He said, painter, you're going to do the-- all right, sure.
I said, boss, but I need flour.
If you need flour, you are clever and go to the kitchen to the Jewish-- and tell them that you need flour.
So I went [? to ?] the [? boss ?] kitchen.
I didn't need nothing because I want flour to make, you know?
And after, I have Polish friends.
And I was [? smuggling. ?] I can't tell you.
I was like a king in his home.
And so the Polish give me, my friends.
I-- I met my friends [INAUDIBLE] the same school, the same class as me.
Oh, a big smoker-- I was smoking every day.
I took a box of cigarettes, take a one.
How big-- was the factory larger or smaller compared to the Emalia factory?
It was-- we have a production of Emalia-- Emalia, just arms.
Should be arm, but we didn't produce it.
But also a little bit smaller.
But the Jewish, they went to England.
But Herr Schindler, I don't know how he managed to take over again.
From October until May-- eighth of May.
They have nothing to do with SS.
They were local in the factory.
They-- they got-- before, it was textile fabric factory.
And they-- they made pullover underwear.
And what a thing to do!
When they came, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] it was-- was sleeping on the straw.
In three, four weeks, everybody got lice.
But which-- which kind of lice?
One day, they didn't let us in because we were in factory, you know?
On the-- rooms-- rooms-- rooms, big rooms, they didn't let us in.
They took every clothes from us, groups.
They put in high [INAUDIBLE] oven-- steam.
And they-- they damaged every lice, everything that was possible.
And they went shower for us.
Hair, everything, up-- and it was everything.
We come upstairs-- new beds, new blankets, new mattress, everything new.
Now, the privilege every day was a shower.
The privilege was maybe 10 people to get-- I was [INAUDIBLE] have a beautiful shower.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] So it was doctors, and we have never saw lice anymore.
This was [? red ?] [? pine ?] and green powder.
Between the powder, I had tobacco, a piece of bread.
I was during-- working during the day.
And in the night, [INAUDIBLE] other people.
When I came in the morning, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] somebody told me, you know, [INAUDIBLE] he want [INAUDIBLE].
He was looking in new cases about this.
And I-- I saw and they steal me some.
And I knew who is the [INAUDIBLE] one.
And not because he [? steal ?] me-- the people told me he came from Tarnow, what is another town.
And he was after the Jews, gone.
They were looking for Jews where they're hiding.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] And I hit him.
And director-- I said, director-- director listen.
And you know what happened to the [INAUDIBLE]?
After the war, he went to Krakow, and they arrest him, the Polish, and they hang him.
Do you remember [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] celebrating anybody's birthday when you were in the factories?
[INAUDIBLE] say I don't remember which one.
You know, like [? holidays. ?] Everyone makes [? half ?] bread [INAUDIBLE].
No, no, no, no Shabbat-- nothing.
This was factory for the arms.
Outside, the SS would shoot-- this is the-- the-- the duty was looking that nobody was going to run away.
But inside, they had nothing-- as I said, nothing to say.
They [INAUDIBLE] to do, because it was cold, and they [INAUDIBLE] to pee, you know, very often.
And they couldn't go out for themselves.
One SS woman should take them out.
But they were [? bastard. ?] They didn't want, you know?
And this was one officer from Wehrmacht what is there from the arms production.
And he said, what is going on?
Provokativ, ?] the-- the-- maybe 20 women wanted to go to [INAUDIBLE].
And the-- the SS refuse the women.
And he took the two women, come with me.
And he went to the-- there was big trouble, but he said, ah, they must producing arms [INAUDIBLE].
It was a fight between SS and Wehrmacht.
It was the end of the war.
And in the morning, 8 May, director said, 3 o'clock, I'm going to put radio.
And everybody's going to listen to radio.
It was [? pick of Churchill, ?] unconditional capitulation.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] And Schindler had a speech.
And they told us, we know what happened.
They killed innocent people, millions of them.
But please, not everyone is guilty.
And please, don't take any, you know, how-- how you say-- get back at them, you know?
Let them through the courts and behave you like human being.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] And when-- when he was speaking, I was jumping [? on him ?] and kissing him, and--
The guards-- just one day before, they went.
They came-- there was one SS man with us.
He stayed, but he was a Pollack.
When the Russians came, immediately, what are you doing?
And they proved this is OK, [INAUDIBLE] let them go.
But there was a group maybe for 12 people.
They came to us two months before the war finished.
This was 15 Jews and one German.
And the German was all prisoner in concentration camp because he was professional murder.
And these people, these 15 people, told us they went kilometers, kilometer.
And this German killed a lot of Jewish prisoner because for bread, you know?
When they were finished, this prisoner, what was his-- they cut him.
And the Russian commander, I don't know, high ranking, report with the SS there, where he killed, how he killed.
And he said, OK, do what you-- what you want.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And it was a big machine.
And they took him to the machine, make a-- you know, like-- had to hang him.
I was-- [LAUGHS] they hanged him, they fell down.
They killed him [INAUDIBLE] like blood animal.
[INAUDIBLE] And the Russian came, what are you doing?
He gave him two in the face.
Give me that revolver, you bastard, [? you! ?] They behave like animal.
I'm going to tell you, the Jews is [? coming ?] Russian officer's women with [INAUDIBLE].
Come with me to the Jews.
We're going to rope the German.
They went to German village, [INAUDIBLE] German.
Do you remember when Schindler left the factory?
It was 8 of May, after his speech.
He told me, come with me because I'm going.
I want to go away because the Russian coming.
And I haven't got a driver.
My future brother-in-law was a driver.
His sister, her sister [INAUDIBLE] his future, she said, OK.
I said, director is going to [?
OK. ?] And he took her revolver.
Please, look after me before I'm going.
But be careful, because if Marta is here, [INAUDIBLE].
She gave me the picture because I wanted something.
We'll come onto that when we look at the pictures.
And I'm regretting that didn't [INAUDIBLE] see here again and again.
I was in German, he was in Israel [INAUDIBLE].
My wife [INAUDIBLE], my son, my son.
You are the son of the [? martyr, ?] of the painter.
No, I don't know nothing about.
Normal-- normal clothes with his wife, with 15 Jews together.
What happened after I know from my sister in [INAUDIBLE].
When they went, they were Russian already.
They give him the hat and [INAUDIBLE] something like that [INAUDIBLE].
[PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] Not [INAUDIBLE] last word, the situation-- I wanted help him to rescue him.
He was looking how-- somehow to get out.
Because he knows that the Russian is coming.
And then the Russian going to cut him, is going to hang him.
To American [? soil. ?] He know there's American there, yeah.
But he-- he went to the border of Switzerland, yeah, towards Germany.
Jewish people, Jewish people-- always Jewish people, yeah.
After the war, he went to Argentine.
He joined and this is another story.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Howe we say-- another.
I know-- I-- I knew the names-- Pinkus, Esther; Richard Drecken.
Who was-- Dora, Dora, Dora, [INAUDIBLE] I forgotted the name.
When the Russian came, I had my friend with his wife.
We took a small house, and we were-- food, the check they give us, food.
And they prepare a train to Krakow, [INAUDIBLE] beautiful for everyone, bed and food.
I took my my-- my-- you know-- my [INAUDIBLE].
[INAUDIBLE] Czechoslovakia and some clothes what Schindler gave us when he left for everyone, you know?
For coats, for-- when they came to Krakow, I said, [?
I am from Krakow-- you stay here in the station.
I wanted to see what-- I walked, I walked, I walked.
Police patrol, police solider and Russian-- stop.
I said, I'm from concentration camp.
Oh, you are from [INAUDIBLE], all right.
A-- a horse without [? that. ?] It's a taxi.
I came from concentration-- in Polish.
My wife in-- in the station.
If you want, take me there and take me there.
Maybe I'm going to find some friend who is going to pay you.
We came to our place where I was living from before.
OK, so my cousin with our friend got one room with a kitchen.
So we went for two weeks and we went to Czechoslovakia again.
I went to my-- and this was a widow with her children.
But the husband was killed for the-- the German killed him.
And the same was in my wife's place.
